jokes.text: The mountain is a majestical place
jokes.setup: Someone trying to sound profound about skiing
jokes.punchline: The malapropism 'majestical' instead of 'majestic'
jokes.explanation: Classic IASIP malapropism, economical and character-specific

dialogue.line: The mountain is a majestical place

jokes.text: What do I do with my hands? How do I ski? Well, you're supposed to have poles. Uh... You know what, hold on to these guys for balance.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking for basic skiing instruction
jokes.punchline: Being told to hold onto glass bottles instead of ski poles for balance
jokes.explanation: Absurd solution that sets up visual comedy and danger

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What do I do with my hands? How do I ski?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Well, you're supposed to have poles. Uh... You know what, hold on to these guys for balance.

jokes.text: These are glass. Yeah. And don't break 'em, bitch.
jokes.setup: Charlie realizing he's holding glass bottles as ski poles
jokes.punchline: Casual threat about not breaking them while skiing down a mountain
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity with casual aggression about an impossible task

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: These are glass.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: And don't break 'em, bitch.

jokes.text: Wonder how Charlie's doing. (Charlie screaming) Oh, here he comes.
jokes.setup: Someone casually wondering about Charlie
jokes.punchline: Immediate answer via Charlie's terrified screaming as he presumably crashes down the mountain
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure - the question is answered immediately by the screaming

dialogue.line: Wonder how Charlie's doing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: (Charlie screaming)
Oh, here he comes.

jokes.text: That's 'cause it's mountain rules, man. I don't get that, though. I just feel like I got lucky.
jokes.setup: Someone explaining Charlie survived because of 'mountain rules'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logical confusion about this nonsensical concept
jokes.explanation: Charlie being the voice of reason in an absurd situation is classic IASIP

dialogue.line: That's 'cause it's mountain rules, man.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't get that, though. I just feel like I got lucky.

jokes.text: While you're back there, could you slap a sticker on the side of mine that says 'pussy'?
jokes.setup: Someone asking for helmet customization
jokes.punchline: Wanting to label themselves as cowardly on their safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor with ironic twist on safety gear

dialogue.line: While you're back there, could you slap a sticker on the side of mine that says 'pussy'?

jokes.text: Part of what makes skiing so fun is that your brains could get bashed in, in any moment.
jokes.setup: Explaining what makes skiing enjoyable
jokes.punchline: The constant threat of traumatic brain injury as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures the show's twisted logic

dialogue.line: Part of what makes skiing so fun is that your brains could get bashed in, in any moment.

jokes.text: Eat this, bitch! (laughing) Oh, shit. What'd you do that for? Oh, sorry, Frank. We thought you were some jerk-off who bought the mountain. I am the jerk-off who bought the mountain.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dee attacking someone they think is a corporate villain
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing he actually is the corporate villain they're opposing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Frank's matter-of-fact delivery of being exactly what they hate

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Eat this, bitch!

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: (laughing) Oh, shit. What'd you do that for?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry, Frank. We thought you were some jerk-off who bought the mountain.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I am the jerk-off who bought the mountain.

jokes.text: I'm turning it into a members-only resort for millionaires to plow their trophy whores in private.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his business plan
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and blunt description of high-end resort activities
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank - completely inappropriate but perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm turning it into a members-only resort for millionaires to plow their trophy whores in private.

jokes.text: Whoa! That sounds amazing, Frank. I'm in. I'm in.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his vulgar resort plan
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately being excited about the explicit sexual commerce
jokes.explanation: Dennis's immediate enthusiasm for Frank's gross plan is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Whoa! That sounds amazing, Frank. I'm in. I'm in.

jokes.text: I-I'm, like, so lost. So, uh, who do I go with in this situation?
jokes.setup: Charlie observing the group split into factions
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about choosing sides like it's a video game
jokes.explanation: Charlie treating real-life conflict like a game mechanic choice is endearing

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I-I'm, like, so lost. So, uh, who do I go with in this situation?

jokes.text: That's my roommate, Turkey. We call him Turkey, 'cause his favorite drink is Wild Turkey.
jokes.setup: Introduction to a character nicknamed Turkey
jokes.punchline: The extremely literal and unimaginative reason for the nickname
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan delivery of mundane nickname origin

dialogue.line: That's my roommate, Turkey. We call him Turkey, 'cause his favorite drink is Wild Turkey.

jokes.text: No, I was in court all day. I was trying to win back custody of my daughter. Oh. Mm. (chuckles): That's a bit of a downer. Yeah. Yeah, pretty much.
jokes.setup: Someone explaining they were in court for custody
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dee's awkward reaction and the guy's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment handled with typical IASIP awkwardness

dialogue.line: No, I was in court all day. I was trying to win back custody of my daughter.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Mm.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's a bit of a downer.
Yeah. Yeah, pretty much.

jokes.text: Who stole my goddamn burrito? No, it's there, Roach. I just moved it to make room for my coleslaw. Look. Did you mess with it? Did you come in it? Did you come in my burrito?!
jokes.setup: Roach looking for his burrito
jokes.punchline: Escalating from simple theft accusation to extremely specific sexual paranoia
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from normal to completely unhinged paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Roach
dialogue.line: Who stole my goddamn burrito?
No, it's there, Roach. I just moved it to make room for my coleslaw. Look.

dialogue.speaker: Roach
dialogue.line: Did you mess with it? Did you come in it? Did you come in my burrito?!

jokes.text: I didn't come in your burrito! I wouldn't do that to you!
jokes.setup: Roach's sexual burrito paranoia
jokes.punchline: Sincere, emotional denial of burrito ejaculation
jokes.explanation: The earnest denial of such an absurd accusation makes it funnier

dialogue.line: I didn't come in your burrito! I wouldn't do that to you!

jokes.text: He's a little squirrelly from the concussions. Like I said, no helmets back in the day.
jokes.setup: Roach's bizarre paranoid behavior
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation that he has brain damage from skiing injuries
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that recontextualizes his behavior as tragic brain damage

dialogue.line: He's a little squirrelly from the concussions. Like I said, no helmets back in the day.

jokes.text: When was the last time you even had a babe in here? Oh, uh... my daughter stopped by about six weeks ago. Served me a court summons. I say count it.
jokes.setup: Mac asking about women visiting
jokes.punchline: Guy counting his daughter serving legal papers as a 'babe visit'
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion where legal troubles become 'female contact' - expertly crafted

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: When was the last time you even had a babe in here?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Oh, uh... my daughter stopped by about six weeks ago. Served me a court summons.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I say count it.

jokes.text: Rent money! No, no. No, no, no, no, no. Whoa, no, no. Drink 'em. Drink 'em.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dee about to destroy aluminum cans
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea to not destroy their only source of income
jokes.explanation: Pathetic moment showing how desperate their financial situation is

dialogue.line: Rent money!
No, no. No, no, no, no, no. Whoa, no, no. Drink 'em. Drink 'em.

jokes.text: Yeah, I thought this was made out of candy. It's a plastic tree, Charlie. It's not made out of candy. You said that this was a candy model. I never said it was a candy model. I believe I heard you mention it was a model made out of candy.
jokes.setup: Charlie eating something from Frank's model
jokes.punchline: His insistence that someone said it was candy despite no evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie logic - creating false memories to justify his actions

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought this was made out of candy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's a plastic tree, Charlie. It's not made out of candy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You said that this was a candy model.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I never said it was a candy model.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I believe I heard you mention it was a model made out of candy.

jokes.text: It'd be a good idea to get a model of the banquet. We could make that candy. I'd like to get a visual on that banquet. And we may as well make it candy. It should be candy.
jokes.setup: Charlie's candy model obsession from previous beat
jokes.punchline: His complete fixation on making everything candy despite being wrong
jokes.explanation: Charlie doubling down on his delusion shows his characteristic persistence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It'd be a good idea to get a model of the banquet. We could make that candy. I'd like to get a visual on that banquet. And we may as well make it candy. It should be candy.

jokes.text: Tatiana, I want you to clean yourself, for I will enter you hard and deep, and it will last for as long or as short as I please. But you will be clean. Only when you are clean will you know my power.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking to an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Extremely creepy, overly dramatic sexual monologue
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis creepiness delivered with complete seriousness - uncomfortable but hilarious

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Tatiana, I want you to clean yourself, for I will enter you hard and deep, and it will last for as long or as short as I please. But you will be clean. Only when you are clean will you know my power.

jokes.text: Everybody get your tits out! (cheering) Hell yes! He just said, 'Take your tits out,' and they all just took their tits out. Yeah, I'm gonna keep mine in, just for now.
jokes.setup: Someone yelling for women to expose themselves
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual decision to not participate in the group nudity
jokes.explanation: Dee's matter-of-fact response to the absurd situation is perfectly deadpan

dialogue.line: Everybody get your tits out!
Hell yes!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He just said, 'Take your tits out,' and they all just took their tits out.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm gonna keep mine in, just for now.

jokes.text: Uh, I lost one of 'em. Prostate thing. You know. Yeah, yeah, me, too. It's still bouncing around in there, but it don't do nothing.
jokes.setup: Old friends catching up after 30 years
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of lost testicles and sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny male bonding over medical problems, Frank's description is particularly vivid

dialogue.speaker: Drisko
dialogue.line: Uh, I lost one of 'em. Prostate thing. You know.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, me, too. It's still bouncing around in there, but it don't do nothing.

jokes.text: Where'd these guys come from? Switzerland? Yeah, but they came here and they're racing with us? I don't... I don't understand that at all.
jokes.setup: Charlie questioning the appearance of Dennis's Swiss racing team
jokes.punchline: His logical confusion about how/why they're there
jokes.explanation: Charlie questioning the show's own absurd logic is meta and funny

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Where'd these guys come from? Switzerland? Yeah, but they came here and they're racing with us? I don't... I don't understand that at all.

jokes.text: You showed me some real stuff out there, guy. I was falling. You were falling, but you were fast. But I crashed into some tables.
jokes.setup: Dennis praising Charlie's skiing
jokes.punchline: Reframing Charlie's crashes as positive because he was 'fast'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's ability to spin complete failure as success is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You showed me some real stuff out there, guy. I was falling. You were falling, but you were fast. But I crashed into some tables.

jokes.text: Looks like Charlie Kelly's walking off the mountain and out of the big race, ladies and gentlemen. He knows my name!
jokes.setup: PA announcer narrating Charlie's actions
jokes.punchline: Charlie's amazed reaction that the announcer knows his name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Charlie notices the absurd omniscient narration, perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: PA Announcer
dialogue.line: Looks like Charlie Kelly's walking off the mountain and out of the big race, ladies and gentlemen.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He knows my name!

jokes.text: Looks like Charlie Kelly's walking off the mountain and out of the big race, ladies and gentlemen. He knows my name!
jokes.setup: PA announcer narrating Charlie's actions
jokes.punchline: Charlie's horror that the announcer knows his name
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the PA announcer absurdity - Charlie realizing the impossible surveillance.

dialogue.speaker: PA Announcer
dialogue.line: Looks like Charlie Kelly's walking off the mountain and out of the big race, ladies and gentlemen.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He knows my name!

jokes.text: We're drilling a hole in the women's shower so we can look at 'em naked. Yeah. Why? What-what does that have to do with Frank and Dennis? I don't know. I don't know. Well, nothing, really. It's just a warm-up prank to get the juices flowing.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dee explaining their 'prank war' plan
jokes.punchline: Admitting their voyeurism has nothing to do with the supposed prank war
jokes.explanation: Shows how the gang justifies terrible behavior with nonsense logic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're drilling a hole in the women's shower so we can look at 'em naked.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. Why? What-what does that have to do with Frank and Dennis?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't know. I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, nothing, really. It's just a warm-up prank to get the juices flowing.

jokes.text: Well, the real prank is when I jam my dick through that hole and yell, 'Special delivery!'
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about peeping through holes
jokes.punchline: Escalating to sexual assault while treating it like a harmless prank
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation typical of the show but relies heavily on shock value

dialogue.line: Well, the real prank is when I jam my dick through that hole and yell, 'Special delivery!'

jokes.text: Look, where I come from, you know, jamming your dick through a hole in the wall, that-that's assault, that's a felony, and, uh, it's just plain wrong.
jokes.setup: The gang's casual discussion of sexual crimes
jokes.punchline: Charlie being the voice of basic human decency
jokes.explanation: Charlie being surprisingly the most normal person is a great character moment

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Look, where I come from, you know, jamming your dick through a hole in the wall, that-that's assault, that's a felony, and, uh, it's just plain wrong.

jokes.text: Freeze! Police! David Drisko, you're under arrest. This man has committed a litany of sexual assaults. His semen was recovered from crime scenes all over the mountain.
jokes.setup: Drisko being set up as the sympathetic underdog
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's actually a serial sex offender
jokes.explanation: Shocking twist that recontextualizes the entire 'Party Mountain' nostalgia

dialogue.speaker: Police
dialogue.line: Freeze! Police! David Drisko, you're under arrest. This man has committed a litany of sexual assaults. His semen was recovered from crime scenes all over the mountain.

jokes.text: This guy might not be as cool as we thought he was. Even his awesome hair is bullshit.
jokes.setup: Learning Drisko is a sex offender and seeing he wears a wig
jokes.punchline: Mac focusing on the hair deception rather than the crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac being more upset about fake hair than sexual assault

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This guy might not be as cool as we thought he was. Even his awesome hair is bullshit.

jokes.text: So you gonna race for them? No, Dennis, I'm gonna race for everyone. I want to race for the people. You see, last night Tatiana taught me all about Communism.
jokes.setup: Charlie deciding to enter the race
jokes.punchline: Sudden conversion to communism via prostitute education
jokes.explanation: Absurd political awakening combined with sexual bragging is peak Charlie

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: So you gonna race for them? No, Dennis, I'm gonna race for everyone. I want to race for the people. You see, last night Tatiana taught me all about Communism.

jokes.text: Cold, hard winters, gross soups. She also taught me a lot about sex. Sweet sex, amazing sex, a lot of sex, Jacuzzi sex, Dennis!
jokes.setup: Charlie describing his communist education
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to detailed sexual bragging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie escalation from politics to sex with increasing specificity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Cold, hard winters, gross soups. She also taught me a lot about sex. Sweet sex, amazing sex, a lot of sex, Jacuzzi sex, Dennis!

jokes.text: Uh... actually, uh... the whole Snow Palace thing was, uh, all for show. I needed to distract the local environmentalist groups while I fracked the mountain.
jokes.setup: The entire episode's conflict and competition
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing it was all a distraction for environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recontextualization of the entire episode - classic IASIP subversion of expectations

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Uh... actually, uh... the whole Snow Palace thing was, uh, all for show. I needed to distract the local environmentalist groups while I fracked the mountain.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I hired the hookers, so you could rip their tops off.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh... That makes a lot of sense.

jokes.text: I hired the hookers, so you could rip their tops off
jokes.setup: Frank revealing the whole setup

jokes.text: So, did you even hire that guy? You know, Charlie, I have no idea who that guy is.
jokes.setup: The omniscient PA announcer throughout the episode
jokes.punchline: Frank admitting he doesn't know who's been narrating their lives

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, did you even hire that guy?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You know, Charlie, I have no idea who that guy is.

jokes.text: I guess some things really are different on the mountain. Yeah.

jokes.punchline: Charlie finally accepting the absurd logic after everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend that brings Charlie's arc full circle with acceptance of absurdity

dialogue.line: I guess some things really are different on the mountain.
